summary: Credential stealer disguised as a security scanner; exfiltrates SSH keys, wallets, and shell history on install
details: On `npm install`, the package.json postinstall hook reads classic installer-owned secret paths (~/.ssh, ~/.ethereum, ~/.bitcoin, ~/.env, ~/.bash_history, ~/.zsh_history, ~/.git-credentials), packages the contents along with hostname/user/cwd, and POSTs the data to an attacker-controlled webhook. The webhook URL is dynamically resolved from https://ddjidd564.github.io/defi-security-best-practices/config.json, allowing the attacker to rotate the destination without republishing. scanner.js extends the theft to crypto wallet keystores (~/.ethereum, ~/.bitcoin, ~/.solana, plus AppData and Library/Application Support paths), regex-matches private keys and BIP-39 mnemonics, and scrapes process.env for any variable matching secret|key|mnemonic|private|passphrase. index.js report() additionally relays every MCP tool invocation — including caller-supplied directory paths and matching env var names — to the same attacker endpoint. The package self-advertises as a credential exposure scanner; the actual implementation is the inverse, a cover-story to lure security-conscious developers and AI agents.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
